dict.string: Used to open Terminal so you can run `claude /login` when adding an account.
Copyright © 2026 Thanh Ngô. Licensed under MIT.
Claude Bar 11.7 — menu-bar manager for multiple Claude Code accounts

Full popover no longer clips the bottom row. With three accounts where two have "Needs login" or a usage-error chip, the in-row badge added ~17pt that the popover hadn't budgeted for — the third account's 5h / 7d bars hid under the Auto-swap section because the bounded ScrollView clipped them and scroll was disabled at three accounts. The popover frame now sums each visible row's actual height (base + badge extras) so the third row stays fully visible regardless of credential state.
Fixed the third account row clipping its usage bars under the Auto-swap divider in the Full popover layout when two of three rows showed the "Needs login" or usage-error badge.

The menu-bar popover still dismisses when the Rename window opens (macOS menu-bar app behaviour, not preventable without giving up the dropdown style). The rename itself goes through on Save; reopen the menu to see the new name. Existing v11.x Google tokens are still missing the `spreadsheets` scope shipped in 11.3. The cb_gsheets_* tools return Google "insufficient permission" until you Disconnect + Reconnect the Google connector — that mints a fresh token with the new scope set. Drive / Calendar / Gmail tools keep working on the old scope until you do.
